findings.description: 教員 (kyouin/teacher) translated inconsistently within page 463: Student table uses 教员编号/教员 (教员系列), Teacher table uses 教师编号/教师姓名 (教师系列), Join result table uses 教员姓名 (混用). The JP source consistently uses 教員 throughout. ZH alternates between 教员 and 教师 with no principled basis.

evidence.student_table_header_4: 教員番号 → zh: 教员编号
evidence.teacher_table_header_1: 教員番号 → zh: 教师编号
evidence.teacher_table_header_2: 教員名 → zh: 教师姓名
evidence.join_result_header_4: 教員名 → zh: 教员姓名

findings.recommended_fix: Standardize to 教员 throughout (matches glossary entry for 教員 if present) or 教师 throughout; pick one and apply consistently across all three tables on p463.

findings.description: Japanese personal names rendered with inconsistent word order in EN across two tables on the same page. Student table (pre-join) uses family-given order (Yamada Taro, Suzuki Aiko, Kamine Ai). Join result table uses given-family order (Taro Yamada, Aiko Suzuki, Ai Kamine). Same individual names, different conventions within the same page.
findings.recommended_fix: Choose one convention (Western given-family order recommended for EN learners) and apply uniformly across all tables on the page.

findings.description: SQL LIKE pattern literals '%うどん%' and 'うどん%' (JP hiragana) are preserved verbatim in the ZH stem translation. The EN stem translates them to '%udon%' and 'udon%'. ZH and EN diverge in how they handle the literal search string, creating a cross-language inconsistency. ZH also contains JP kana characters inside a ZH field (technically jp-kana-in-zh).

evidence.zh_stem_fragment: 商品名为'%うどん%'所选出的商品数 / 商品名为'うどん%'
evidence.en_stem_fragment: Product Name is '%udon%' / Product Name is 'udon%'

findings.recommended_fix: ZH should romanize or translate the udon literal to match EN: use '%乌冬%' (ZH translation of udon dish) or '%udon%' (romaji). Keeping JP hiragana in a ZH field is inconsistent with EN treatment.

answer_verification.p469_q1: PASS (computed a=4, b=3, c=2 → descending order a,b,c = answer_index 0)

checks_clean.data_integrity_join_result: CORRECT (join rows verified against source tables)

summary: 10 pages audited, 193 leaves checked. 3 MINOR findings: (F1) 教員 zh-translation inconsistency across 3 tables on p463 (教员 vs 教师 mixed); (F2) EN personal name word-order inconsistency within p463 tables (family-given vs given-family); (F3) JP kana literal retained in ZH SQL LIKE patterns on p469 while EN translates to romaji. No U+FFFD, no duplicate IDs, no sentence-as-term defects. 9/10 quiz answers verified correct; 1 unverifiable without figure.
